A wonderful small Khmer cast bronze figure of the Buddha sheltered by a Naga (Buddha Muchalinda), Angkor Period, style of the Bayon, late in the period, 14th century, Cambodia. This small Khmer cast bronze sculpture is known as Buddha Muchalinda, and depicts the Buddha seated in meditation, raised upon the coils of the Naga King Muchalinda, and shelter by his seven heads and hoods which fan out behind the Buddha's head.
